data.title: Intel Shares Pull Back As Weak Q1 Guidance Overshadows Earnings Beat
data.description:
Intel Corporation (NASDAQ:INTC) shares are trading lower Friday. The company reported fourth-quarter financial results on Thursday after the market closed and issued first-quarter adjusted earnings per share guidance below estimates.

Fourth-Quarter Results Beat Expectations

Intel reported adjusted earnings per share of 15 cents, beating the consensus estimate of 8 cents. In addition, the company reported revenue of $13.67 billion, beating the consensus estimate of $13.37 billion.

CEO Lip-Bu Tan said the company made progress on its strategy and highlighted the introduction of its first products built on Intel 18A process technology. CFO David Zinsner added that Intel exceeded fourth-quarter expectations despite industry-wide supply shortages, while demand fundamentals across core markets remain healthy.

First-Quarter Earnings Per Share Outlook Comes in Below Consensus

The company sees revenue of $11.70 billion to $12.70 billion, versus the consensus estimate of $12.49 billion. Furthermore, the company anticipates adjusted earnings per share of 0 cents, versus the consensus estimate of 5 cents.

Analyst Changes: Following the earnings report, multiple analysts issued price target adjustments.

  • Needham analyst N. Quinn Bolton reiterated a Hold rating on Intel.
  • Benchmark analyst Cody Acree maintained a Buy rating on Intel and raised the price target from $50 to $57.
  • Rosenblatt analyst Kevin Cassidy maintained a Sell rating on Intel and raised the price target from $25 to $30.

Technical Analysis Of Intel

Currently, Intel is trading 9.3% above its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 28.4% above its 100-day SMA, indicating that the longer-term uptrend remains intact despite the current pullback. Over the past 12 months, shares have increased 151.83% and are positioned closer to their 52-week highs than lows, suggesting strong overall performance.

The RSI is at 76.74, indicating that the stock is in overbought territory, while the MACD is above its signal line, suggesting bullish momentum. This combination of an overbought RSI and a bullish MACD indicates that while the stock may be experiencing a pullback, there is still underlying strength.

Benzinga Edge Rankings

Below is the Benzinga Edge scorecard for Intel, highlighting its strengths and weaknesses compared to the broader market:

  • Momentum: Bullish (Score: 97.31) — Stock is outperforming the broader market.
  • Value: Neutral (Score: 50.67) — Trading at a fair valuation relative to peers.

The Verdict: Intel’s Benzinga Edge signal reveals a strong momentum score, indicating that the stock is currently outperforming the market. However, the neutral value score suggests that while the stock is performing well, it is not necessarily a bargain at current levels.

INTC Price Action: At the time of writing, Intel shares are trading 16.15% lower at $45.55, according to data from Benzinga Pro.

Image via Shutterstock

data.title: Vaxcyte Pushes Next-Gen Pneumococcal Vaccine Into Phase 3 Phase
data.description:
Vaxcyte, Inc. (NASDAQ:PCVX), with a market capitalization of around $7 billion, is trading higher on Friday.

The stock’s positive movement comes as Vaxcyte is advancing its VAX-31 vaccine trials across adult and infant programs, which could position it as a leader in pneumococcal disease prevention.

VAX-31 is the company’s next-generation 31-valent pneumococcal conjugate vaccine (PCV) candidate.

Vaxcyte Initiates Phase 3 VAX-31 Trials

Vaxcyte on Friday said it dosed the first participants in a Phase 3 OPUS-2 study evaluating VAX-31 in conjunction with a seasonal influenza vaccine in participants aged 50 and above. The study plans to enroll around 1,300 participants.

Additionally, the company plans to initiate another Phase 3 OPUS-3 trial in around 720 adults previously vaccinated against pneumococcal disease in the first quarter of 2026, indicating a strong commitment to expanding its vaccine portfolio.

Topline data from both OPUS-2 and OPUS-3 studies are expected in the first half of 2027.

The company also reported that it has completed enrollment (n=905) for a Phase 2 dose-finding study of VAX-31 in infants.

Commercial Supply Chain

This comprehensive approach reflects Vaxcyte’s strategy to enhance its manufacturing capabilities, including a planned $1 billion investment in U.S. manufacturing and services, starting with a custom fill-finish line in North Carolina in the first quarter of 2026.

Meanwhile, the broader market experienced a mixed session on the previous trading day, with the Russell 2000 down 0.16% and the Nasdaq declining by 0.37%. Vaxcyte’s gains come despite these declines, suggesting that the stock’s movement is driven by company-specific developments rather than broader market trends.

PCVX Shows Strong Short-Term Momentum

The stock is currently trading 13.4% above its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 15.4% above its 100-day SMA, indicating strong short-term momentum. Over the past 12 months, shares have decreased by 40.17%, and they are currently positioned closer to their 52-week highs than lows.

The RSI is at 71.77, which is considered overbought territory, suggesting that the stock may be due for a pullback. Meanwhile, the MACD is above its signal line, indicating bullish momentum.

The combination of overbought RSI and bullish MACD suggests mixed momentum, indicating that while the stock has strong upward pressure, it may also face selling pressure soon.

  • Key Resistance: $55.00
  • Key Support: $50.00

Vaxcyte holds a consensus Buy rating among analysts, with an average price target of $125.00, reflecting strong confidence in its growth potential.

Vaxcyte’s Benzinga Edge Scorecard Overview

Below is the Benzinga Edge scorecard for Vaxcyte, highlighting its strengths and weaknesses compared to the broader market:

  • Momentum: Bullish (Score: 41.19) — Stock is showing some positive trend strength.

The Verdict: Vaxcyte’s Benzinga Edge signal reveals a stock that is currently experiencing bullish momentum but may be approaching overbought territory. Investors should monitor for potential pullbacks while considering the strong growth outlook based on upcoming clinical trials.

Significance: Because PCVX carries significant weight in these funds, any significant inflows or outflows for these ETFs will likely force automatic buying or selling of the stock.

PCVX Price Action: Vaxcyte shares were up 2.06% at $54.94 at the time of publication on Friday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

data.title: This Trade Desk Analyst Is No Longer Bullish; Here Are Top 5 Downgrades For Friday
data.description:

  • Seaport Global analyst Martin Englert downgraded Cleveland-Cliffs Inc. (NYSE:CLF) from Buy to Neutral. Cleveland-Cliffs shares closed at $14.45 on Thursday. See how other analysts view this stock.

  • Bernstein analyst Peter Weed downgraded Confluent, Inc. (NASDAQ:CFLT) from Outperform to Market Perform and announced a $31 price target. Confluent shares closed at $30.50 on Thursday. See how other analysts view this stock.
